free_text: Fission Product Yields for Thermal-Neutron Fission of
Sample contained in small polyethylene container.
Pu-239 fission chamber used to determine the
Detector efficiency calibration made with standard
Calibration checked with Ba-140 and La-140 decay.
Random summing and live-time clock test used Cl-38 and
Yields obtained from data by determining
gamma activity after irradiation, correcting for
detector efficiency and gamma branching ratio, and
dividing by the number of fissions in the sample.
Corrected for detector efficiency and gamma-ray
branching ratio. Some spectra required a correction
-ratio of measured yield of peaks to the decay
Yield of peaks and decay constant errors
This value not recommended for use until 166-keV
OS. Decay data updated, converted to lower
Note by compiler: the 40 min state of Sb-130 is
labelled by authors as metastable state, as also
appearing in literature (e.g. Karlsruhe chart of
nuclides of 2007). more recently (2016) this state is
listed as ground state. No isomeric transition.
